symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden tearing/ripping chest pain radiating to the interscapular back

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Tearing, radiating-to-back pain is the classic descriptor of aortic dissection.
Severity, tearing quality and autonomic features are not features of benign chest-wall strain.
Exertional blood-pressure surge is a recognised trigger for dissection in a weakened aorta.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe pounding headache with feeling faint

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute aortic dissection with hypertensive emergency
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Upper-body hypertensive crisis and impending haemodynamic compromise produce headache and pre-syncope.
Non-specific but consistent with haemodynamic compromise; also keeps pulmonary embolism on the differential.
Breathlessness is compatible with PE, which remains a reasonable alternative.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known Turner syndrome with bicuspid aortic valve and coarctation of the aorta

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: This aortopathy triad dramatically raises baseline dissection risk and mandates treating chest pain as aortic until excluded.

symptoms.symptom_name: Arm-versus-leg blood-pressure gradient (right arm 195/110 vs left leg 110/70)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A limb blood-pressure differential indicates aortic obstruction/dissection — clinician-measured, sealed; the AI Doctor infers compromise from history, not a measured gradient.
Radio-femoral delay is a hallmark of aortic coarctation and of flow obstruction — requires palpation, not available in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Harsh systolic ejection murmur over the aortic area radiating to the back

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Consistent with a bicuspid aortic valve / aortic flow turbulence — requires auscultation, sealed from telehealth.
Sympathetic surge / early shock; visible on video or reported by the roommate, so observable in telehealth though not clinician-measured.

investigation_details.expected_result: Intimal flap / false lumen confirming aortic dissection and defining its extent
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Dissection unconfirmed and surgical planning delayed — but imaging is a hospital investigation, never available in real time via telehealth, so escalation must not wait for it.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: CT demonstration of an intimal flap is confirmatory — obtained in hospital after emergency transfer.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Tearing interscapular pain + upper-body hypertensive features + a known Turner/bicuspid-valve/coarctation aortopathy together make dissection the leading diagnosis and mandate immediate emergency escalation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: CT aortography (in hospital, after emergency transfer)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms and characterises the dissection to plan surgery — arranged by the receiving cardiothoracic team, not by the telehealth clinician.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: 12-lead ECG and troponin (to assess coronary involvement in hospital)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Helps exclude/assess concurrent coronary involvement in hospital; must NOT delay escalation and must not lead to empirical antiplatelet therapy before dissection is excluded.
Contraindicated on suspicion of aortic dissection — would promote catastrophic bleeding. Listed to encode the error of commission.
